channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: I tried out 3 media outlets’ AI chatbots. Here’s what happened
item.description: How fitting for Time to debut an AI chatbot last week with its annual Person of the Year package. AI was almost certainly Thing of the Year, after all, even if there’s no official award for that.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Looking for small drones? Listen for the right radio signals
item.description: The recent spate of unidentified drone sightings in the U.S., including some near sensitive locations such as airports and military installations, has caused significant public concern. Some of this recent increase in activity may be related to a September 2023 change in U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: SAP customers still slow to deploy AI broadly
item.description: A new survey of SAP customer organizations shows that, despite AI experimentation, few have implemented AI and generative AI technologies across their enterprises.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Nissan and Honda to attempt a merger that would create the world's No. 3 automaker
item.description: Japanese automakers Nissan and Honda have announced plans to work toward a merger that would catapult them to a top position in an industry in the midst of tectonic shifts as it transitions away from its reliance on fossil fuels.
item.dc:creator: Mari Yamaguchi And Elaine Kurtenbach, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Chris Christie predicts Elon Musk's influence over Trump will end
item.description: Former New Jersey Gov. Chris Christie (R) predicted in a Sunday interview that tech billionaire Elon Musk’s influence over President-elect Trump will end. “When does this end?” ABC News’s Jonathan Karl asked Christie on “This Week” of Musk’s influence on Trump.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Google TV users are getting even more free channels in time for the holidays
item.description: If you're in the US, you now have a total of 171 channels to watch through the Google TV Freeplay app.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: NASA's Parker Solar Probe aims to fly closer to the sun like never before
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A NASA spacecraft aims to fly closer to the sun than any object sent before. The Parker Solar Probe was launched in 2018 to get a close-up look at the sun. Since then, it has flown straight through the sun's corona: the outer atmosphere visible during a total solar eclipse.
item.dc:creator: ADITHI RAMAKRISHNAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Bluesky finds with growth comes growing pains — and bots
item.description: Bluesky has seen its user base soar since the U.S. presidential election, boosted by people seeking refuge from Elon Musk’s X, or wanting an alternative to Meta’s Threads and its algorithms.
item.dc:creator: Ali Swenson And Barara Ortutay, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Google Whisk: A New AI Tool for Visual Concept Creation
item.description: Google's new AI tool, Whisk, uses images as inputs instead of text-based prompts to create and remix visual concepts. Learn how to try it out and what you can expect from this experimental tool.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: These are the companies using AI-driven dynamic pricing the most - and the top users probably won't surprise you
item.description: Amazon leads dynamic pricing with 12.6 daily changes, but Best Buy changes its price only 2.6 times per day.
item.dc:creator: udinmwenefosa@gmail.com (Efosa Udinmwen), TechRadar

item.title: Amazon and Starbucks workers are on strike. Trump might have something to do with it
item.description: Amazon delivery drivers and Starbucks baristas are on strike in a handful of U.S. cities as they seek to exert pressure on the two major companies to recognize them as unionized employees or to meet demands for an inaugural labor contract.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Arm’s high-stakes licensing suit against Qualcomm ends in mistrial, but Qualcomm prevails in key areas
item.description: Arm has lost a battle over licensing of its microprocessor designs to Qualcomm, ending doubt over the immediate future of some of the chip maker’s products.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Amazon workers are striking at multiple facilities. Here's what you should know
item.description: Amazon workers affiliated with the Teamsters union continued a strike Friday at seven of the company's delivery hubs just days before Christmas. At midnight on Saturday, workers at a prominent unionized warehouse in New York will also join, the Teamsters said.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Arm awaits verdict in high-stakes licensing suit against Qualcomm
item.description: Arm is waiting to see if it has won a battle over licensing of its microprocessor designs to Qualcomm that has cast doubt over the immediate future of some of the chip maker’s products.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Mainframe modernization that meets you where you are
item.description: Every business is unique in its approach to technology modernization. Likewise, the reasons behind initiating a modernization strategy will vary from business to business. However, a few key drivers have emerged as a common thread between businesses.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: VA EHR to resume rollout in mid-2026, after clinicians see fewer crashes
item.description: The VA says clinicians are seeing fewer interruptions from the new EHR, and that the average user now sees “near zero interruptions,” such as freezes or lags. The post VA EHR to resume rollout in mid-2026, after clinicians see fewer crashes first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: Transparency, efficiency, innovation: The digital transformation of policing in Kazakhstan
item.description: In the era of digital transformation, public safety stands at a critical crossroads. Law enforcement agencies globally are under increasing scrutiny to enhance transparency, efficiency, and trust within their communities.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Amazon workers strike at multiple facilities as Teamsters seek labor contract
item.description: Workers at seven Amazon facilities went on strike Thursday, an effort by the Teamsters union to pressure the e-commerce company for a labor agreement during a key shopping period.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Giant sloths and mastodons lived with humans for millennia in the Americas, new discoveries suggest
item.description: New discoveries from several archaeological sites in North and South America suggest that ancient people first arrived in the New World much earlier than scientists once thought.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Australian who falsely claimed to have invented bitcoin is found in contempt of UK court
item.description: LONDON (AP) — An Australian computer scientist who falsely claimed to be the founder of the bitcoin cryptocurrency was found Thursday to be in contempt of an order of London's High Court and was sentenced to 12 months in prison, suspended for two years.
item.dc:creator: PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Massive data breach at federal credit union exposes 240,000 members
item.description: A credit union with over 240,000 members recently revealed it was targeted by cybercriminals, resulting in a data breach that was part of a two-month attack by hackers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Apple complains Meta requests risk privacy in spat over EU efforts to widen access to iPhone tech
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple complained that requests from Meta Platforms for access to its operating software threaten user privacy, in a spat fueled by the European Union's intensifying efforts to get the iPhone maker to open up to products from tech rivals.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: YouTube’s top trends for 2024 include Bay Area creators
item.description: Online video giant YouTube has published its year-end top trends lists for 2024 and in includes Bay Area content creators.
item.dc:creator: Pueng Vongs, The Mercury News

item.title: Flexibility or security? Trick question: It’s a false choice. Here’s what to do instead
item.description: Remember when 'work' was a place? Federal agencies rethink BYOD to balance security and usability. The post Flexibility or security? Trick question: It’s a false choice. Here’s what to do instead first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: How the Trump administration is likely to deal with artificial intelligence
item.description: The National Defense Authorization Act for 2025 has extensive language on ensuring competition in AI. What can we expect from the Trump administration? The post How the Trump administration is likely to deal with artificial intelligence first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: CompTIA launches expert-level cybersecurity certification
item.description: CompTIA this week made available its CompTIA SecurityX certification program, which is an updated version of the CompTIA Advanced Security Practitioner (CASP+) cert and part of CompTIA’s Xpert Series of certifications.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Barcelona subway recycles energy from braking into power to charge electric cars
item.description: BARCELONA, Spain (AP) — The Barcelona subway grinds to a halt, the doors slide open and commuters pour out to go about their daily business. Little do they know that as they do so, a burst of energy is sent up to street level to help charge an electric car.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH WILSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: You have a license to code
item.description: One of the coolest things about software development is that you don’t need anyone’s permission to do it. If you need an application for your business or personal use, there isn’t a single thing stopping you from learning to code and writing it yourself.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The push to upskill the technology workforce in federal agencies
item.description: The federal government is facing a massive tech talent shortage, particularly as the development and use of AI continues to gain traction. The post The push to upskill the technology workforce in federal agencies first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Senate report alleges Amazon rejected warehouse safety recommendations due to productivity concerns
item.description: At least two internal Amazon studies found a link between how quickly the online retailer's warehouse workers perform tasks and workplace injuries, but the company rejected many safety recommendations out of concern the proposed changes might reduce productivity, according to a U.S.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, Fox 5
